title: Skill
description: A packaged reusable skill: instructions plus optional scripts and evals, graduated from a pattern that recurred enough times across runs to deserve a name. Skills are never invented from scratch; they are extracted from observed practice.

id.description: Skill identifier in kebab-case. Example: voice-lint or weekly-portfolio-audit.

version.description: Semantic version. A breaking change to instructions, trigger surface, or script API requires a major bump.

owner_guild.description: Team or guild accountable for maintaining the skill and reviewing its evals.

trigger.description: Phrases or situations that should invoke the skill. Used by the harness or human reviewer to decide when the skill applies.

instructions_file.description: Path to the markdown file containing the skill's instructions, relative to the repo root.

scripts.description: Executable helpers shipped with the skill. May be empty for instruction-only skills.

name.description: Short script name used in the skill instructions.

path.description: Path to the script file, relative to the repo root.

description.description: One-line description of what the script does.

evals.description: Eval cases that exercise the skill. May be empty for early-stage skills, but promotion past proposed status should require at least one passing eval.

path.description: Path to the eval file or directory.

promotion_policy.description: Conditions that must hold before the skill can graduate from proposed to active.

requires.description: Gates the skill must pass. At least one must be listed.
